name: NMFC Freight Classes by Density (13-sub FCDC scale)
description: LTL freight class bands derived from shipment density (lb/ft^3) under the NMFC Freight Classification Density Chart's 13-subprovision structure (NMFC Docket 2025-1, effective 2025-07-19). Density-based classes are an estimator only: many commodities carry fixed NMFC classes, and handling, stowability, and liability can override density. Confirm classifications with NMFTA ClassIT+ or your carrier.
review_frequency: annually (NMFTA docket cycle; re-check after each NMFC docket disposition)
source: NMFTA 'Decoding Density' article (nmfta.org/news/decoding-density-the-freight-factor-you-cant-afford-to-overlook/, subs 1-10) and NMFTA Help Center FAQ 'Can you share with us the density and class breaks for the new 13-sub structure' (help.nmfta.org, explicitly confirming subs 11-13). Both checked 2026-07-02 via corpus-logistics-supply-chain/sourced-reference-data-2026-07-02.md.
sourcing_notes: 13-subprovision FCDC density scale per NMFC Docket 2025-1, effective 2025-07-19; supersedes the legacy 18-class density ladder previously stubbed here. Classes range 50-500 overall, but the density chart itself tops out at class 400 (density < 1 pcf). Density-based subprovisions do not govern all commodities: many items carry fixed classes, and handling, stowability, liability, commodity description, packaging, and carrier inspection can affect classification. ClassIT+ is the official lookup path. Any calculator output must be labelled an approximate estimate.
